title: Cluster review page
analysis: Build the cluster review page at /review/clusters with card grid, merge, relabel, and exclude functionality. The codebase has a well-established pattern: review.py contains both page routes (/review/*) and JSON API routes (/api/*), with HTMX-aware dual responses (HTML partial vs JSON). Templates use Tailwind dark mode with surface-* colors, and cards have predictable IDs ({entity}-{id}) for HTMX outerHTML swaps. The activity_clusters table exists with cluster_id, label, description, time_start, time_end, location_label, gps coords, and clip_ids_json. Missing from db.py: (1) single-cluster getter get_activity_cluster(id), (2) delete_activity_cluster method, (3) 'excluded' column on the table, (4) column whitelist on update_activity_cluster (it currently does raw **kwargs into SQL like the narrative update did before task 46 fixed it). The review hub already maps waiting gates to review links via _REVIEW_LINKS dict — needs 'classify' entry. Navigation in base.html already links to /review/clusters. New test file test_cluster_review.py keeps cluster tests separate from the 678-line test_review.py. All cluster routes go in the existing review.py following the task spec and narrative route convention.

prerequisites.description: Add 'excluded INTEGER DEFAULT 0' to activity_clusters schema in CatalogDB.__init__ — add to CREATE TABLE statement for new DBs and add ALTER TABLE with try/except OperationalError for existing DBs that lack the column

steps.description: Write failing tests for get_activity_cluster(id): returns dict for existing cluster with all fields, returns None for missing cluster. Also test that excluded column defaults to 0 on newly inserted cluster.

Implement get_activity_cluster(cluster_id) in CatalogDB — SELECT * FROM activity_clusters WHERE cluster_id=? returning dict(row) or None. Add excluded column to CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE migration. Pattern follows get_narrative().
Write failing tests for delete_activity_cluster(id): cluster is removed after delete (get returns None), no error when deleting missing id.
Implement delete_activity_cluster(cluster_id) in CatalogDB — DELETE FROM activity_clusters WHERE cluster_id=?

Write failing test that update_activity_cluster rejects columns not in whitelist (raises ValueError with descriptive message), while accepting all valid columns (label, description, time_start, time_end, location_label, gps_center_lat, gps_center_lon, clip_ids_json, excluded).

Add _CLUSTER_ALLOWED_COLUMNS frozenset to CatalogDB and whitelist validation to update_activity_cluster, following the _NARRATIVE_ALLOWED_COLUMNS pattern. Allowed columns: label, description, time_start, time_end, location_label, gps_center_lat, gps_center_lon, clip_ids_json, excluded.
Write failing tests for GET /api/clusters: returns JSON list of clusters with clip_ids parsed from clip_ids_json and clip_count added; returns empty list when no clusters exist.
Implement api_list_clusters route and _parse_cluster helper in review.py. _parse_cluster pops clip_ids_json, parses to clip_ids list, adds clip_count. Pattern follows _parse_narrative.
Write failing tests for GET /api/clusters/{cluster_id}: returns single cluster JSON with parsed clip_ids and clip_count; returns 404 for missing cluster.
Implement api_get_cluster route in review.py — get_activity_cluster(id), parse, return JSON or raise 404.

Write failing tests for POST /api/clusters/{cluster_id}/relabel: updates label and description, returns updated cluster JSON; 404 for missing cluster; rejects extra fields in body via Pydantic validation (422).

Implement api_relabel_cluster route with ClusterRelabel Pydantic model (label: Optional[str], description: Optional[str], extra='forbid'). Calls update_activity_cluster with provided fields, commits, returns updated cluster.

Write failing tests for POST /api/clusters/{cluster_id}/exclude: sets excluded=1, returns updated cluster with excluded=1; 404 for missing cluster; idempotent (excluding already-excluded cluster succeeds).
Implement api_exclude_cluster route — calls update_activity_cluster(id, excluded=1), commits, returns updated cluster JSON.

Write failing tests for POST /api/clusters/merge: (a) merges 2 clusters — combined clip_ids_json, label from largest cluster, min(time_start)/max(time_end), merged cluster kept, other deleted; (b) merges 3 clusters; (c) returns 422 for fewer than 2 cluster_ids; (d) returns 404 if any cluster_id not found.

Implement api_merge_clusters route with MergeRequest(cluster_ids: list[str]) Pydantic model. Logic: validate >=2 ids, load all clusters (404 if any missing), find largest by clip count, combine all clip_ids, compute min time_start/max time_end, update largest cluster with combined data, delete others, commit, return merged cluster.
Write failing tests for GET /review/clusters HTML page: returns 200 with cluster card labels and clip counts visible; empty state shows 'No clusters' message; excluded clusters show 'excluded' badge.

Create review/clusters.html template (extends base.html) with card grid, merge checkboxes, Merge Selected button, and Approve Gate button. Create partials/cluster_card.html with cluster info, status badge, relabel/exclude buttons. Implement clusters_page route in review.py.

Write failing tests for HTMX responses: POST relabel with HX-Request:true returns HTML containing div with id='cluster-{id}' and updated label; POST exclude with HX-Request:true returns HTML partial with 'excluded' badge.

Add _render_cluster_partial helper (renders partials/cluster_card.html). Wire relabel and exclude routes to return HTML partial when _is_htmx(request) is true, JSON otherwise. Pattern follows _render_narrative_partial.

Write failing test for review hub integration: when classify gate is 'waiting', hub page contains card with 'classify' stage, link to /review/clusters, and pending cluster count (non-excluded clusters).

Update _REVIEW_LINKS to add 'classify': '/review/clusters'. In review_hub route, add classify stage handling: count non-excluded activity clusters as pending_count with 'activity clusters' as pending_label.

design_decisions.decision: Add 'excluded INTEGER DEFAULT 0' column to activity_clusters rather than a separate exclusion tracking table
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec suggests a boolean field directly on the table. SQLite integer-boolean is the project norm. A column on the existing table is simpler than a join table, and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN is safe for SQLite with a DEFAULT value. Try/except OperationalError handles existing DBs gracefully.

design_decisions.decision: Add column whitelist to update_activity_cluster following the _NARRATIVE_ALLOWED_COLUMNS pattern from task 46
design_decisions.rationale: The current update_activity_cluster interpolates raw **kwargs keys into SQL (same vulnerability task 46 fixed for narratives). Adding a frozenset whitelist is defense-in-depth. All non-PK columns are allowed: label, description, time_start, time_end, location_label, gps_center_lat, gps_center_lon, clip_ids_json, excluded.

design_decisions.decision: Merge keeps the largest cluster (by clip count) as the surviving record and deletes the others
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec says 'pick label from largest cluster' and 'delete merged clusters, keep the combined one'. Keeping the largest cluster's row preserves its cluster_id, which may be referenced in narratives via activity_cluster_ids_json. Updating in-place avoids orphaned references.

design_decisions.decision: Create separate test file tests/test_cluster_review.py rather than appending to test_review.py
design_decisions.rationale: test_review.py is already 678 lines focused on narrative review. Cluster review is a distinct feature area with its own fixtures (_seed_cluster), DB method tests, and API tests. Separate files keep both manageable and allow parallel test execution.

design_decisions.decision: All cluster routes go in review.py following the task spec and existing convention
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec says 'extend autopilot/web/routes/review.py'. The file already mixes /review/* page routes and /api/* JSON routes for narratives. Adding /review/clusters and /api/clusters/* follows the same pattern. No new router registration needed.

design_decisions.decision: _parse_cluster adds computed clip_count field alongside clip_ids
design_decisions.rationale: Avoids template-side array length calculation. The API response includes both the full clip_ids list and a convenience clip_count integer. This is cheap to compute and makes the card template simpler.

design_decisions.decision: Merge endpoint returns 422 for fewer than 2 cluster_ids, 404 if any cluster not found
design_decisions.rationale: Merging requires at least 2 clusters by definition. Using 422 (Unprocessable Entity) for validation errors and 404 for missing resources follows HTTP semantics and the project's existing error handling patterns in gates.py and review.py.

design_decisions.decision: Hub counts non-excluded clusters as pending for classify gate
design_decisions.rationale: Excluded clusters are intentionally removed from consideration, so they shouldn't inflate the pending count. This gives the reviewer an accurate picture of how many clusters still need review before approving the classify gate.

reuse.how: Reuse directly for all new cluster routes — same DB connection pattern and HTMX detection. No duplication needed since they're in the same file.
Create _parse_cluster following identical pattern: pop clip_ids_json, json.loads to list, add clip_count = len(clip_ids).
Create _render_cluster_partial using same templates.get_template + template.render + HTMLResponse pattern, targeting partials/cluster_card.html.

reuse.what: Pydantic request body models with ConfigDict(extra='forbid')
reuse.how: Create ClusterRelabel(label, description) and MergeRequest(cluster_ids) Pydantic models with same extra='forbid' config for strict input validation.
Create _CLUSTER_ALLOWED_COLUMNS frozenset with identical validation logic: check kwargs keys against whitelist, raise ValueError listing bad keys.

reuse.what: Test fixture pattern: file-backed DB, create_app, TestClient, _seed_* helper
reuse.how: Create analogous fixtures in test_cluster_review.py: db fixture with tmp_path, app/client fixtures, _seed_cluster helper inserting cluster with sensible defaults.

reuse.what: narratives.html template layout (card grid, bulk actions, gate approval section)
reuse.how: clusters.html follows identical structure: extends base.html, card grid with grid-cols-1/2/3, action header with Merge Selected button, Approve Gate section at bottom.
cluster_card.html uses same pattern: div#cluster-{cluster_id} as swap target, hx-post/hx-target/hx-swap='outerHTML' on action buttons, status badges with Tailwind color classes.

reuse.what: Review hub _REVIEW_LINKS dict and pending count logic
reuse.how: Add 'classify': '/review/clusters' to _REVIEW_LINKS. Add elif stage == 'classify' block to count non-excluded clusters, mirroring the narrate stage pattern.

reuse.what: Existing CatalogDB activity_clusters CRUD (insert, get_all, clear, update)
reuse.how: Routes use get_activity_clusters() for list, update_activity_cluster() for relabel/exclude. New get_activity_cluster(id) and delete_activity_cluster(id) extend the existing CRUD section.
